en: A Safety Management System (SMS) is a systematic and explicit approach outlining all the activities undertaken by an organization in order to achieve acceptable or tolerable safety levels.
A program that outlines the expectations of individuals that serve in a managerial role within an organization.
A program that encourages staff members to assist in the decision-making process and to supply feedback for improving work processes.
Knowing who is responsible for the different aspects of the job.
Accountability & responsibility for managers, supervisors & employees
Ensuring that all the necessary items have been put in place so you have met the minimum legal requirements.
Resources for meeting health & safety requirements
A program that details how to review the health and safety performance of the employees.
A program that establishes protocols to analyze accidents or incidents and determine a root cause and corrective actions.
Accident Reporting, Investigation & Root Cause Analysis
A program that establishes protocols for the enforcement of organizational rules and regulations, as well as related legislation.
A program that establishes protocols in the event of an emergency.  This provides appropriate actions or steps to take, which parties should be contacted, and designates meeting locations.
Identifying hazards in the workplace and putting control measures to make them safe
Conducting a Hazard Analysis, i.e. Job Safety Analysis(JSA), Job Hazard Analysis(JHA), Task Hazard Analysis(THA)
(e.g. safety awareness of large animals, poisonous plants, insects and spiders, snakes, diseases from animals, etc.)
Any behavior that is intended to disturb or upset another employee, and is normally repetitive.
Policies, Procedures, and/or Safe Work Practices
This outlines what is expected from each employee.
Safety responsibilities for managers, supervisors & workers
A program that outlines methods for managers to communicate internal information within an organization.
How and when you update your policies and procedures making sure that everything is still relevant and applicable.
Continuous evaluation and revision of policies and procedures
Examples of programs: New employee, Green Hand, mentor program, etc.
New Employee or equivalent mentoring program for inexperienced workers
Identifying who has the power/authority to stop work activities due to health and safety issues

Please answer the questions below accordingly:

"Yes" if your company currently has a programme.
"No" if your company should have a programme but does not.
"NA" if your company is not required to have a programme.
Safety and Environmental Systems (SEMS) - Offshore Safety
